name: Activity Feed & Cost Tracking Pages
description: Activity feed full page: chronological event list auto-updating via SSE (useEventSource hook), filterable by agent/project/event type. Cost & Token dashboard page: per-project and per-agent cost breakdown bar charts, token usage over time line charts (recharts), budget alert configuration stored in SQLite. Gracefully shows 'no data available' placeholder UI when token/cost data is missing from filesystem. Overview/home dashboard page that aggregates: agent count + status summary cards, active projects count, PACT health snapshot, last 10 activity events, cost burn rate.

types.name: ActivityEventType
types.description: Discriminant values for the ActivityEvent discriminated union. Each variant maps to one event source.

types.name: ActivityEvent

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique event identifier (UUID v4)

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminant: determines the shape/semantics of the payload

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 datetime string (UTC). Parsed to Date in domain layer.

fields.name: agent_id
fields.description: Agent that produced or is subject of this event. Null if not agent-scoped.

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: Project context. Null if not project-scoped.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Human-readable one-line summary of the event

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Variant-specific structured data. Keys/values depend on 'type' discriminant.

types.description: A single activity event in the feed. Uses 'type' as the discriminant field for the discriminated union. All events share common fields; variant-specific payload is in 'payload' as a typed dict.

types.name: OptionalStr
types.description: Explicit nullable string — null means 'not applicable', not 'unknown'.

types.name: OptionalInt
types.description: Explicit nullable integer — null means 'no data available'.

types.name: OptionalFloat
types.description: Explicit nullable float — null means 'no data available'.

types.name: ActivityFilter

fields.name: agent_id
fields.description: Filter to events involving this agent

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: Filter to events in this project

fields.name: event_types
fields.description: Filter to these event types. Empty list = all types.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Cursor for pagination — opaque string (event ID of last seen item)

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Max events to return per page. Clamped to [1, 50].

types.description: Filter criteria for the activity feed. All fields optional — omitted fields mean 'no filter on this dimension'. Stored in URL searchParams for shareability.

types.name: ActivityEventTypeList
types.description: List of activity event type filters

types.name: ActivityEventList
types.description: Ordered list of activity events (chronological descending)

types.description: Time granularity for aggregating cost and token time-series data

types.name: CostScope
types.description: Dimension to group cost breakdown by

types.name: CostSummary

fields.name: entity_id

fields.name: period_end

types.description: Aggregated cost breakdown for a single entity (project or agent). Monetary values in integer cents to avoid floating-point issues.

fields.name: timestamp

fields.name: output_tokens

fields.name: entity_id

fields.name: period

fields.name: threshold_cents

validators.error_message: Threshold must be a positive integer in cents

fields.name: enabled

types.description: Zod-validated input for creating a new budget alert. id/created_at/updated_at are auto-generated.

types.description: Aggregated data for the overview/home dashboard page. Each section is independently nullable so widgets can render their own error/empty states when fetched via Promise.allSettled.

types.name: ApiSingleResponse

fields.name: data
fields.description: The response payload. Null if resource not found or error occurred. Actual type is contextual per endpoint.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Error message if the request failed. Null on success.

types.description: Consistent API response envelope for single-resource endpoints: { data: T | null, error?: string }

types.name: ApiCollectionResponse

fields.name: data
fields.description: Array of items. Empty array if no results. Actual item type is contextual per endpoint.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Opaque cursor for next page. Null if no more pages.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Error message if the request failed. Null on success.

types.description: Consistent API response envelope for collection endpoints: { data: T[], cursor?: string }

types.name: SSEEventPayload

fields.name: event_type
fields.description: Maps to SSE event: field for client-side addEventListener

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Full event object serialized as JSON in SSE data: field

types.description: Structure of SSE push events. The SSE 'event:' field = type discriminant; 'data:' field = JSON serialized ActivityEvent.

types.name: RealtimeBuffer

fields.name: events
fields.description: Buffered events, newest first

fields.name: capacity
fields.description: Maximum number of events to hold in buffer

validators.error_message: Buffer capacity must be between 1 and 1000

fields.name: overflow_count
fields.description: Number of events dropped due to buffer overflow since last drain

types.description: Bounded in-memory buffer for real-time events held between SSE pushes. Max 500 events.

functions.name: getActivityEvents
functions.description: Retrieves a paginated, filtered list of activity events from the filesystem data layer. Returns events in reverse chronological order. Uses cursor-based pagination. Never throws for missing/malformed data — returns empty array.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Filter and pagination criteria. All fields optional with sensible defaults.

error_cases.name: invalid_filter
error_cases.condition: Zod validation of filter params fails

error_cases.name: filesystem_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unexpected I/O error reading event source files (not missing files — those return empty)

preconditions: filter.limit is clamped to [1, 50] before query execution
If filter.cursor is provided, it must reference a valid event ID or is silently ignored (returns from beginning)
postconditions: Returned data array length <= filter.limit
Events are sorted by timestamp descending (newest first)
If data array length < filter.limit, cursor in response is null (no more pages)
All events in response match the provided filter criteria
Empty array returned (not error) when no events match filters

structured_side_effects.description: Reads agent session logs, PACT state files to assemble activity events
Logs parse failures for malformed event files at warn level

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = total events matching filter, capped by pagination

functions.name: getCostSummary
functions.description: Retrieves per-entity cost breakdown data for bar chart rendering. Groups costs by project or agent over a specified date range. Returns pre-aggregated CostSummary items shaped for recharts BarChart.

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: Group by 'project' or 'agent'

inputs.name: period_start
inputs.description: ISO 8601 start of reporting period

inputs.name: period_end
inputs.description: ISO 8601 end of reporting period

error_cases.name: invalid_date_range
error_cases.condition: period_start > period_end or dates are not valid ISO 8601

error_cases.name: invalid_scope
error_cases.condition: scope is not 'project' or 'agent'

error_cases.name: filesystem_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unexpected I/O error reading cost data files

preconditions: period_start is a valid ISO 8601 datetime string
period_end is a valid ISO 8601 datetime string
period_start <= period_end
scope is one of 'project' | 'agent'
postconditions: data field contains CostSummaryList (possibly empty) on success
Each CostSummary has total_cost_cents >= 0
All monetary values are in integer cents (no floating point)
Returns empty list (not null data, not error) when no cost data exists for the period
Summaries are sorted by total_cost_cents descending (highest cost first)

structured_side_effects.target: OpenClaw session cost data on filesystem
structured_side_effects.description: Reads token/cost logs from agent session directories
Logs parse failures for malformed cost files at warn level

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = number of session files in period

functions.name: getCostTimeSeries
functions.description: Retrieves time-bucketed cost data for line chart rendering. Pre-aggregates cost data at the specified granularity. Returns data shaped for recharts LineChart / AreaChart.

inputs.name: granularity
inputs.description: Time bucket size: hourly, daily, weekly, monthly. Defaults to 'daily'.

inputs.name: period_start
inputs.description: ISO 8601 start of reporting period

inputs.name: period_end
inputs.description: ISO 8601 end of reporting period

inputs.name: entity_id
inputs.description: Filter to specific project/agent. Null for global aggregate.

error_cases.name: invalid_date_range
error_cases.condition: period_start > period_end or dates are not valid ISO 8601

error_cases.name: invalid_granularity
error_cases.condition: granularity is not a valid CostGranularity value

error_cases.name: entity_not_found
error_cases.condition: entity_id provided but does not match any known project or agent

error_cases.name: excessive_range
error_cases.condition: Date range at given granularity would produce > 1000 data points

error_cases.name: filesystem_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unexpected I/O error reading cost data files

preconditions: period_start is a valid ISO 8601 datetime string
period_end is a valid ISO 8601 datetime string
period_start <= period_end
granularity is one of 'hourly' | 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly'
postconditions: data field contains CostDataPointList (possibly empty) on success
Data points are sorted by timestamp ascending (chronological order for charts)
Each data point's timestamp aligns to the granularity bucket boundary
Empty time buckets within the range are included with cost_cents = 0 (no gaps in series)
Returns empty list when no cost data exists

structured_side_effects.target: OpenClaw session cost data on filesystem
structured_side_effects.description: Reads token/cost logs for time-series aggregation
Logs parse failures at warn level

performance_budget.complexity: O(n + b) where n = session files, b = output buckets

functions.name: getTokenUsageTimeSeries
functions.description: Retrieves time-bucketed token usage data (input + output tokens) for line chart rendering. Pre-aggregates at specified granularity. Returns data shaped for recharts with separate input/output series.

inputs.name: granularity
inputs.description: Time bucket size. Defaults to 'daily'.

inputs.name: period_start
inputs.description: ISO 8601 start of reporting period

inputs.name: period_end
inputs.description: ISO 8601 end of reporting period

inputs.name: entity_id
inputs.description: Filter to specific project/agent. Null for global aggregate.

error_cases.name: invalid_date_range
error_cases.condition: period_start > period_end or invalid ISO 8601

error_cases.name: invalid_granularity
error_cases.condition: granularity not a valid CostGranularity value

error_cases.name: entity_not_found
error_cases.condition: entity_id provided but not found

error_cases.name: excessive_range
error_cases.condition: Range would produce > 1000 data points

error_cases.name: filesystem_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unexpected I/O error

preconditions: period_start is a valid ISO 8601 datetime string
period_end is a valid ISO 8601 datetime string
period_start <= period_end
granularity is one of 'hourly' | 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly'
postconditions: data field contains TokenUsageDataPointList (possibly empty) on success
Data points sorted by timestamp ascending
Empty time buckets included with input_tokens = 0 and output_tokens = 0
Token counts are non-negative integers
Returns empty list when no token data exists

structured_side_effects.target: OpenClaw session token usage data on filesystem
structured_side_effects.description: Reads token usage logs for time-series aggregation
Logs parse failures at warn level

performance_budget.complexity: O(n + b) where n = session files, b = output buckets

functions.name: getOverviewData
functions.description: Fetches all overview dashboard sections via Promise.allSettled pattern. Each section is independently nullable so individual widget failures don't break the entire page. Returns OverviewData with per-section null for unavailable data.

error_cases.name: total_failure
error_cases.condition: All data sources simultaneously fail (extremely rare)

postconditions: data field contains OverviewData (never null at top level)
Each sub-field in OverviewData is independently null or populated
If a section's data source fails, that field is null and no error is thrown
recent_events contains at most 10 events
agent_statuses covers all AgentStatusKind variants (count may be 0)
cost_burn_rate.last_24h_cents and last_7d_cents are non-negative

structured_side_effects.target: OpenClaw agent configs, session data, PACT state files
structured_side_effects.description: Reads multiple filesystem sources to assemble overview
Logs individual section failures at warn level for debugging

performance_budget.complexity: O(a + p + e + c) where a=agents, p=PACT proposals, e=recent events, c=cost records

functions.name: createBudgetAlert
functions.description: Creates a new budget alert in SQLite. Validates input with Zod, generates UUID and timestamps, inserts into budget_alerts table. Returns the created BudgetAlert.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Zod-validated creation payload

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Input fails Zod schema validation (missing fields, invalid types, threshold <= 0)

error_cases.name: duplicate_alert
error_cases.condition: An alert with the same scope + entity_id + period combination already exists

error_cases.name: entity_not_found
error_cases.condition: entity_id does not reference a known project or agent

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite write operation fails unexpectedly

preconditions: input passes Zod schema validation
input.threshold_cents > 0
input.entity_id is non-empty
SQLite database is initialized with budget_alerts table
postconditions: data field contains the created BudgetAlert with generated id, created_at, updated_at
BudgetAlert.id is a valid UUID v4
BudgetAlert.created_at == BudgetAlert.updated_at (new record)
Record is persisted in SQLite and retrievable by id
If enabled, alert is immediately active for threshold evaluation

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts a new row into the budget_alerts table
Logs alert creation at info level

functions.name: updateBudgetAlert
functions.description: Updates an existing budget alert in SQLite. Only provided fields in the update input are modified. Updates the updated_at timestamp.

inputs.name: alert_id
inputs.description: UUID of the budget alert to update

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Partial update payload — only provided fields are changed

error_cases.name: not_found
error_cases.condition: No budget alert exists with the given alert_id

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Update input fails Zod validation

error_cases.name: empty_update
error_cases.condition: No fields provided in update input

error_cases.name: duplicate_alert
error_cases.condition: Update would create a duplicate scope + entity_id + period combination

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite write operation fails unexpectedly

preconditions: alert_id is a valid UUID v4 string
At least one field in input is provided (non-empty update)
If input.threshold_cents is provided, it must be > 0
SQLite database is initialized with budget_alerts table
postconditions: data field contains the updated BudgetAlert
BudgetAlert.updated_at is set to current UTC timestamp
BudgetAlert.created_at is unchanged
Only fields present in input are modified; other fields retain previous values
Changes are persisted in SQLite

structured_side_effects.description: Updates an existing row in the budget_alerts table
Logs alert update at info level

functions.name: deleteBudgetAlert
functions.description: Deletes a budget alert from SQLite by ID. Idempotent — deleting a non-existent alert returns 404 (not an error in the crash sense, but a client error).

inputs.name: alert_id
inputs.description: UUID of the budget alert to delete

error_cases.name: not_found
error_cases.condition: No budget alert exists with the given alert_id

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite delete operation fails unexpectedly

preconditions: alert_id is a non-empty string
postconditions: data field contains the deleted BudgetAlert (for confirmation UI)
Record is removed from SQLite budget_alerts table
Alert is no longer evaluated for threshold breaches
Subsequent getBudgetAlert(alert_id) returns null

structured_side_effects.description: Deletes a row from the budget_alerts table
Logs alert deletion at info level

functions.name: getBudgetAlert
functions.description: Retrieves a single budget alert by ID from SQLite. Returns null data if not found.

inputs.name: alert_id
inputs.description: UUID of the budget alert to retrieve

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite read operation fails unexpectedly

preconditions: alert_id is a non-empty string
postconditions: data field contains BudgetAlert if found, null if not found
No error field set when alert simply doesn't exist (data is null)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure SQLite read — no side effects beyond query execution

functions.name: listBudgetAlerts
functions.description: Lists all configured budget alerts from SQLite. Optionally filtered by scope and/or entity_id. Returns empty array if none configured.

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: Filter by scope type

inputs.name: entity_id
inputs.description: Filter by specific entity

error_cases.name: invalid_scope
error_cases.condition: scope provided but not a valid BudgetAlertScope value

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite read operation fails unexpectedly

postconditions: data field contains BudgetAlertList (possibly empty)
If scope filter provided, all returned alerts match that scope
If entity_id filter provided, all returned alerts match that entity_id
Alerts sorted by created_at descending (newest first)
cursor is always null (no pagination needed — budget alerts are bounded)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure SQLite read — no side effects beyond query execution

functions.name: evaluateBudgetAlerts
functions.description: Checks all enabled budget alerts against current spending data. For each alert whose threshold is exceeded, emits a cost_threshold_exceeded ActivityEvent via the SSE event bus. Called periodically by the data layer (not directly by API routes).

error_cases.name: database_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite read of alerts fails

error_cases.name: sse_publish_failure
error_cases.condition: Failed to publish event to SSE bus (non-fatal — alert still returned in list)

preconditions: SQLite budget_alerts table is initialized
Cost data is accessible on filesystem
SSE event bus is initialized and accepting events
postconditions: Returns list of BudgetAlerts that exceeded their threshold in the current evaluation
For each exceeded alert, a cost_threshold_exceeded ActivityEvent has been published to SSE bus
Alerts that are not enabled are skipped
Alerts that were already triggered in the current period are not re-triggered (deduplication)
No exceptions thrown for missing cost data — those alerts simply don't trigger

structured_side_effects.target: OpenClaw session cost data on filesystem
structured_side_effects.description: Reads cost data to compare against thresholds
Publishes cost_threshold_exceeded events for exceeded alerts
Logs threshold evaluations and exceeded alerts at info level

performance_budget.complexity: O(a * n) where a = number of enabled alerts, n = cost records per alert period

functions.name: subscribeToActivityStream
functions.description: Establishes an SSE connection for real-time activity events. Returns a ReadableStream that pushes ActivityEvent payloads. Supports optional filtering by event type, agent, or project via query parameters. Maintains connection with periodic heartbeats.

inputs.name: event_types
inputs.description: Filter to specific event types. Empty = all types.

inputs.name: agent_id
inputs.description: Filter to events for this agent. Null = all agents.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: Filter to events for this project. Null = all projects.

error_cases.name: sse_bus_unavailable
error_cases.condition: SSE event bus is not initialized or crashed

error_cases.name: invalid_filter
error_cases.condition: Filter parameters fail validation

preconditions: SSE event bus is initialized
Client supports text/event-stream content type
postconditions: Returns text/event-stream response with Content-Type header
Each SSE message has event: field set to ActivityEventType value
Each SSE message has data: field containing JSON-serialized ActivityEvent
Heartbeat comment sent every 30 seconds to keep connection alive
Connection cleaned up when client disconnects (AbortSignal)
Only events matching the provided filters are sent to this client

structured_side_effects.description: Subscribes to the shared event bus with optional filters; holds open HTTP connection
Logs connection open/close events at debug level

invariants: All monetary values are represented as non-negative integer cents (int >= 0) throughout the entire contract — no floating-point currency
All token counts are non-negative integers (int >= 0)
All timestamps are ISO 8601 UTC strings in serialized/API form
Null explicitly means 'data not available' or 'not applicable'; empty arrays mean 'no results found' — these are semantically distinct
ActivityEvent.type discriminant fully determines the valid keys in ActivityEvent.payload
API responses always use the envelope pattern: ApiSingleResponse for single resources, ApiCollectionResponse for lists
Budget alert uniqueness constraint: no two alerts may share the same (scope, entity_id, period) tuple
The real-time event buffer never exceeds 500 events; oldest events are evicted when capacity is reached
All filesystem reads use Zod .safeParse() — parse failures are logged and result in null/empty returns, never exceptions
Components never read files or query SQLite directly — all access goes through lib/data/ layer functions defined in this contract
The overview page fetches all sections via Promise.allSettled — each widget independently handles its error/empty state
Activity feed filter state is stored in URL searchParams for shareability and browser back/forward support
SSE heartbeat comments are sent every 30 seconds to prevent proxy/load-balancer timeout
Cost time-series include zero-value buckets for time periods with no activity to prevent chart gaps
Pagination uses cursor-based approach with opaque cursor strings (event IDs), max 50 items per page
requires: lib/sse/ - SSE event bus for real-time push
lib/db/ - SQLite schema + typed query wrappers for budget alerts
lib/data/ - Data access layer (filesystem + SQLite) — components never read files or query SQLite directly
lib/openclaw/ - OpenClaw config/session reader for agent and project data
lib/pact/ - PACT CLI wrapper + file parser for health snapshots
recharts - charting library for cost/token visualizations
zod - input validation for all API params and file parsing
